display_name: Apple Macintosh Classic / Plus (9" 1-bit white monitor, 1984-1990)

source.notes: All Compact Macs (128K, 512K, Plus, SE, SE/30, Classic, Classic II) used the same 9" CRT with a 1-bit (pure black/white) framebuffer at 512x342. Greys were created entirely through dithering — there were no analog grey levels. This profile mirrors that by setting posterize_levels=2 so a modern desktop captured by Tubelight gets quantised to literal black-or-white with the cool-white P4-class phosphor tint.
P4 white phosphor used in compact Macs. Cool-white tint, short persistence (~0.5 ms representative).
